decks.notes: The deck focuses on utilizing the Oath of Druids card to quickly put powerful creatures onto the battlefield, while controlling the game with counterspells, removal, and disruptive cards. Wrenn and Six helps with card advantage and graveyard recursion, while Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Oko, Thief of Crowns provide additional threats and disruption. The deck also includes various utility lands like Boseiju, Who Endures and Strip Mine, along with mana accelerants like Black Lotus and the Moxen. Overall, the deck aims to quickly establish board presence and control the game against opponents in the Vintage 1 vs 1 format.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by using Oath of Druids to put powerful creatures into play quickly, while also having access to disruptive spells like Force of Will and Force of Negation to protect your game plan. With the ability to cheat out creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers early in the game, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats while maintaining control of the board through counterspells and other disruptive elements. Additionally, the deck includes card draw and manipulation to ensure consistency in executing its game plan efficiently.

The deck focuses on quickly deploying powerful artifacts to disrupt the opponent and overwhelm them with aggressive threats. It aims to control the board using cards like Wasteland, Sphere of Resistance, and Trinisphere while generating advantage through mana acceleration from Mishra's Workshop and artifacts like Mana Crypt and Moxen. The deck utilizes Golos, Tireless Pilgrim to search for key artifacts and lands while also incorporating graveyard recursion with Crucible of Worlds. Additionally, it features utility cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Mystic Forge to provide additional versatility and card advantage.

This Vintage 1 vs 1 Control-Oath of Druids deck focuses on using the Oath of Druids enchantment to cheat big creatures into play, such as Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers. The deck also includes powerful counter spells like Force of Will and Force of Negation to protect its creatures and disrupt the opponent's game plan. With cards like Show and Tell and Flash to put creatures into play quickly, along with card draw and mana acceleration, this deck aims to control the game and overwhelm the opponent with its powerful threats.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with dredge cards like Golgari Grave-Troll and Stinkweed Imp, utilizing the deck's self-mill mechanics to bring back powerful creatures like Ichorid and Prized Amalgam. By using cards like Bazaar of Baghdad and Serum Powder, the deck seeks to effectively dig through the deck and set up powerful combos. Additionally, control elements like Force of Will and Chalice of the Void help protect the deck's game plan, while cards like Creeping Chill provide additional reach and pressure on the opponent.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful control spells, card draw, and card advantage to disrupt the opponent's game plan while efficiently deploying threats and protecting them. The deck includes powerful mana accelerants like Black Lotus and Moxen to quickly establish board presence and mana advantage. With a mix of card draw spells, counterspells, and disruptive artifacts, the deck aims to control the game's pace and ultimately outvalue the opponent using efficient threats like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Snapcaster Mage. The companion Lurrus of the Dream-Den adds an additional layer of resilience and recurring threats to the deck.

The deck focuses on using Paradoxical Outcome to generate card advantage and mana by returning artifacts to hand, then replaying them for additional value. The key strategy is to control the board with counterspells and removal while setting up a big turn with Paradoxical Outcome to draw cards and generate mana to combo off and win the game. The deck also utilizes powerful artifact mana sources like Black Lotus, Mana Crypt, and Moxen to ramp into its combo pieces quickly. It aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents with efficient plays and powerful synergy between its cards.

The key concept of this deck is to control the game through powerful spells and abilities, while also utilizing artifacts and creatures to maintain board presence and advantage. The deck focuses on a combination of counterspells, card draw, and artifact synergies to disrupt the opponent's strategy and eventually win by either overwhelming them with powerful threats like Karn, the Great Creator or utilizing Time Vault combos. The deck also includes efficient mana acceleration and card selection to ensure consistency in executing its game plan.

The deck focuses on assembling the combination of Doomsday and Thassa's Oracle to win the game instantly. It includes various card draw and manipulation spells to set up the combo quickly, as well as disruption in the form of Force of Will and other counterspells to protect the combo. The deck also utilizes fast mana like Black Lotus and Dark Ritual to accelerate its game plan, while including other powerful cards like Ancestral Recall and Demonic Tutor for additional consistency and flexibility. The goal is to efficiently combo off and win the game before the opponent can respond effectively.

This deck aims to quickly assemble the Doomsday combo, using a combination of fast mana, card draw, and disruption to win the game in a single turn. By stacking the deck with Doomsday and Thassa's Oracle, the player can draw their whole library and win the game on the spot. The deck also includes counter spells and cantrips to protect the combo. The key to success with this deck is to efficiently set up the combo pieces and protect them until the crucial turn to win the game.

This deck aims to disrupt the opponent while establishing a strong board presence through aggressive artifact creatures and mana denial with cards like Wasteland, Sphere of Resistance, and Crucible of Worlds. The deck also utilizes powerful artifacts like Moxen and Sol Ring to ramp into threats like Golos, Tireless Pilgrim and Lodestone Golem. With access to cards like Trinisphere, Chalice of the Void, and Null Rod, the deck has multiple ways to slow down the opponent's game plan and take control of the game. Additionally, cards like Inventors' Fair and Urza's Saga provide additional utility and card advantage to keep the deck ahead in resources.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and mana denial to disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure with aggressive creatures. It aims to quickly ramp into powerful threats like Golos, Tireless Pilgrim and Karn, the Great Creator while disrupting the opponent's mana base with cards like Trinisphere and Wasteland. The deck also utilizes synergy between artifacts like Nettlecyst and Mystic Forge to further increase its pressure on the opponent. Overall, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and disruptive artifacts.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of powerful spells, card advantage, and resource denial. It includes a variety of counterspells to disrupt the opponent's plans, along with powerful draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Stock Up to maintain card advantage. The deck also features key artifacts like Time Vault and Lórien Revealed to help lock down the game and secure a win condition. By efficiently managing resources and disrupting the opponent, the deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver the opponent to secure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to cheat out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Sphinx of the Steel Wind onto the battlefield using cards like Show and Tell and Tinker. The deck also utilizes mana acceleration from cards like Moxen and Ancient Tomb to cast these powerful creatures quickly. Void Mirror is used to disrupt opponent's spells while Orcish Bowmasters and Mai, Scornful Striker provide additional creature removal and aggression. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with its powerful creatures and disruptive spells, ultimately securing victory through its combo strategy.

The deck focuses on disrupting opponents with cards like Force of Will and Collector Ouphe while generating value with cards like Deathrite Shaman and Leovold, Emissary of Trest. It also provides card advantage through spells like Ancestral Recall and Dig Through Time. The deck aims to control the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, while also putting pressure on the opponent with aggressive creatures like Psychic Frog. The mana base is designed to support the deck's multicolor strategy and allow for flexible casting of spells.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through powerful cards like Force of Will, Show and Tell, and Oath of Druids. By utilizing ramp cards like Black Lotus and Boseiju, Who Endures, the deck aims to cheat out high-impact creatures like Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers and Atraxa, Grand Unifier. The deck also includes disruption and card draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Gitaxian Probe to maintain board presence and outvalue the opponent. With a strong mix of control elements and powerful threats, the deck aims to dominate the game and win through overwhelming advantage.
